| `RESCUES` | Dramatic single-moment extraction from danger — rescuer saves the target from death, capture, or peril. Distinct from `PROTECTS` (steady-state guardianship) and `HEALS` (medical restoration). Covers Beric rescuing BWB members, Davos rescued by Salladhor, Sam & Gilly rescued by the Mysterious Rider, Jon rescuing Ghost from wildlings. Tier-3 (no qualifier). | Rescuer → Rescued-person | corpus |
| `TORTURES` | The act of inflicting deliberate physical pain — flaying, racking, burning, or other sustained bodily torment. Distinct from `ATTACKS` (generic physical violence; not necessarily sustained or systematic), `ASSAULTS` (sexual violence specifically), `EXECUTES` (formal death sentence), and the `REVEALS_TO=under_torture` qualifier (which captures the informational *effect* of torture, not the act itself). Major narrative thread: Bolton flaying tradition, Mountain at Harrenhal, Qyburn's "studies", the Bloody Mummers, Black Cells. Without this edge, "who has Ramsay tortured?" cannot be graph-answered. Tier-3 (no qualifier; method is sub-typology the narrative captures). | Torturer → Tortured-person | corpus |
| `AGENT_IN` | Acts as the agent/executor of an event — the participant who actually performed the act. Source is the executor; target is the event node. Reification role edge: use when attaching a character or house to a multi-party event hub instead of (or in addition to) a dyadic binary edge. See `VICTIM_IN` (patient), `COMMANDS_IN` (orderer who did not personally execute), `WIELDED_IN` (instrument). | Person/House → Event (`event.*`) | — |
| `VICTIM_IN` | Receives the action of an event as the victim or patient — the participant on whom the act was performed. Source is the recipient; target is the event node. Reification role edge: complement to `AGENT_IN` on the same event hub. | Person/House → Event (`event.*`) | — |
| `WITNESS_IN` | The **observer/perceiver** slot on an event hub — a character whose *load-bearing perception* of a charged event (violence, death, a secret, an atrocity) is itself worth a graph edge, distinct from acting (`AGENT_IN`), suffering (`VICTIM_IN`), ordering (`COMMANDS_IN`), or combatting (`FIGHTS_IN`). **Default to an EDGE** onto the existing event node (the Q2 three-lens consensus, S117): reify the witnessing as its own `event.*` NODE *only* when the act of seeing owns an outgoing causal edge — i.e. the seeing `CAUSES`/`TRIGGERS`/`MOTIVATES` a downstream event (e.g. `bran-witnesses-jaime-and-cersei`, whose seeing causes his fall) — or the witnessed thing has no node of its own. Mechanical test: *does the seeing need an outgoing edge? yes → node; no → edge.* Promotion path: start as an edge, promote to a node later only if a causal edge actually gets drawn. **Text-anchor gate:** emit only when the prose shows the character actually SEES it — present-but-shielded does NOT qualify (Yoren forces Arya's eyes shut at Ned's execution → Arya is NOT a witness; Sansa, who "could not turn her head," IS). **Boundary vs `ATTENDS`:** `ATTENDS` = voluntary audience at a *staged social/ceremonial gathering* (feast, wedding, tourney-as-a-whole, court, coronation); `WITNESS_IN` = perception of a charged/violent/secret incident. Same person can `ATTENDS` the Tourney of the Hand AND `WITNESS_IN` the Loras-vs-Gregor bout within it (different targets). Tier-1 when verbatim-cited. Added Session 117 (2026-06-21); first instances `sansa-stark WITNESS_IN execution-of-eddard-stark`, `arianne-martell WITNESS_IN myrcella-is-maimed-by-darkstar`, `hoster-tully WITNESS_IN battle-of-the-camps`. | Person → Event (`event.*`) | — |

> **Reification role vocabulary note:** `WIELDED_IN` (already in the Possession & Artifacts section, artifact→event) serves the **instrument** role for reified events — no `INSTRUMENT_IN` type is needed. `COMMANDS_IN` (below) serves the **orderer/instigator** role. Together with `AGENT_IN` and `VICTIM_IN`, these four cover the standard **action/instrument** participant slots (agent, patient, orderer, instrument) on an event hub. The **perceiver/observer** slot is a fifth role, `WITNESS_IN` (S117) — epistemic, not action (the perceiver acts on nothing and receives nothing); voluntary audience at a *staged* gathering is `ATTENDS`, not `WITNESS_IN`.

### Knowledge & Information

> **Deprecated (Session 63, 2026-05-21):** `KNOWS` was removed from the active vocabulary. Stage 4 wiki-prose classification ran an 82.3% fallback rate on KNOWS emits — the semantic boundary ("knows of" vs "met once" vs "heard rumor of") is too blurry for prose-derived classification to enforce. Character-knowledge relationships will be derived from a future Pass-1-based chapter co-occurrence + Information Revealed pass. Existing KNOWS edges in the graph (363 Haiku-emit + 21 Sonnet-control-arm) are preserved as historical record; downstream consumers filter deprecated types on read.

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `IGNORANT_OF` | Critically lacks information that the reader or other characters have | Person → Information | — |
| `SEEKS` | Pursuing knowledge, artifact, or person | Seeker → Sought | — |
| `REVEALS_TO` | Discloses information to another | Revealer → Recipient (note what was revealed) | — |
| `DECEIVES` | Deliberately misleads | Deceiver → Deceived (note the deception) | — |
| `DECEIVED_BY` | Reverse of `DECEIVES` — emitted on target nodes pointing to deceiver | Deceived → Deceiver | — |
| `HOARDS` | Institution or person suppresses knowledge | Hoarder → Knowledge | — |
| `INVESTIGATES` | Actively trying to learn or prove something | Investigator → Subject | — |
| `TEACHES` | Transmits knowledge or skill (general/casual instruction) | Teacher → Student | — |
| `TUTORS` | Sustained formal one-on-one mentorship — narrower than `TEACHES` (Syrio→Arya water-dancing, Cressen→Stannis childhood, Aemon→Sam ravenry, Septa Mordane→Sansa) | Tutor → Student | — |
| `HEALS` | Medical or maester treatment — restoration of body, not resurrection of the dead (which is `RESURRECTS`). Maester Luwin healing Bran after the fall; Aemon healing Sam; the unnamed septon healing Sandor. **Excludes:** Red Priests reviving the dead (use `RESURRECTS`); Qyburn's reanimation of the Mountain (use `RESURRECTS`) | Healer → Healed | — |
| `AFFLICTED_BY` | Character suffers from a named disease, condition, or magical affliction (living state). Target is `concept.medical`. Distinct from `KILLED_BY` (target = person), `DIED_AT` (location), `DIED_OF` (cause-of-death; this is the living state). Examples: Jorah Mormont/greyscale, Shireen Baratheon/greyscale, Stannis/burns. | Character → Medical | — |
| `DIED_OF` | Character's death was caused by a named disease/condition (post-mortem state). Target is `concept.medical`. Distinct from `KILLED_BY` (person-killer), `DIED_AT` (location), `EXECUTED_WITH` (judicial weapon). Mirrors `AFFLICTED_BY` for the post-mortem state. Examples: Hoster Tully/Spring Sickness, Albin Massey/Shivers, Medrick Manderly/Winter Fever, the Old King Jaehaerys/Great Spring Sickness. | Character → Medical | — |
| `SPIES_ON` | A spy or agent actively surveils, monitors, or gathers intelligence about the target. Distinct from `SERVES` (employment state) and `MANIPULATES` (wrong direction — spy works for handler, not against target). Westeros has structurally-distinct spy networks (Varys's little birds, Littlefinger's network, Qarth's whispers). Tier-3. | Person → Surveilled-person | n/a (Pass 1 corpus + Sonnet no-fit) |
| `INFORMS` | A spy, agent, or informant reports intelligence to their handler or spymaster. Complements `SPIES_ON` (target = person being watched vs. target = handler receiving reports). Distinct from `SERVES` (too generic) and `REVEALS_TO` (one-time disclosure; INFORMS is the ongoing reporting relationship). Tier-3. | Person → Handler/Spymaster | n/a (Pass 1 corpus + Sonnet no-fit) |

### Emotional & Perceptual

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `PERCEIVED_AS` | How POV Character X sees Character Y (with characterization notes) | Perceiver → Perceived | — |
| `TRUSTS` | Places confidence in | Truster → Trusted | — |
| `DISTRUSTS` | Lacks confidence in, suspects | Distruster → Distrusted | — |
| `RESPECTS` | Holds in high regard | Respecter → Respected | — |
| `FEARS` | Afraid of (person, faction, or thing) | Fearer → Feared | — |
| `LOVES` | Deep emotional attachment (familial, romantic, or platonic) | Lover → Loved | — |
| `HATES` | Deep enmity or loathing | Hater → Hated | — |
| `MOURNS` | Grieves for (dead person, lost thing) | Mourner → Mourned | — |
| `PROTECTS` | Acts as guardian or defender | Protector → Protected | — |
| `RESENTS` | Harbors bitterness toward | Resenter → Resented | — |
| `COMPANION_OF` | Close personal friendship or camaraderie. Distinct from `ALLIES_WITH` (political alliance), `TRUSTS` (one-direction confidence), `LOVES` (romantic/deep-familial), `RESPECTS` (cold regard). Use when prose explicitly names a friendship ("good friends with", "sworn brothers", "close companion"). Examples: Patrek Mallister & Edmure Tully; Robert & Ned in their youth; Brienne & Pod; Davos & Salladhor Saan. | Symmetric | — |
| `REPUTED_AS` | Collective reputation or general perception attached to a character without a specific perceiver — distinct from `PERCEIVED_AS` which requires a named POV. Target is `concept.*` (e.g., `concept.magic` for "reputed witch", `concept.craft` for "reputed swordsman", `concept.vice` for "reputed drunkard"). Use when prose narrates a public-domain reputation rather than one character's view of another. | Character → Concept | — |
| `ENCOUNTERS` | Plot-significant face-to-face meeting between two characters, anchored by explicit prose staging (verb gate enforced by validator per CRITICAL RULE — see classify prompt Rule 6). Reserved for first meetings, brief road-crossings, set-piece confrontations short of duel/combat where the meeting itself carries narrative weight. Examples: Dunk ↔ Egg at the Ashford inn; Brienne ↔ Randyll Tarly in AFFC; Arya ↔ Sandor on the kingsroad; Jon ↔ Mance at the Frostfangs; Sam ↔ Coldhands beyond the Wall. NOT for co-presence at events (use `LOCATED_AT` / `ATTENDS` / `FIGHTS_IN`), retinue/court presence (`TRAVELS_WITH`), or formal diplomatic engagement (`NEGOTIATES_WITH`). **Coverage scope (Session 63, 2026-05-21):** Stage 4 captures only wiki-prose-staged meetings — biographical-summary register often elides staging verbs even when meetings happened in-text. Comprehensive character-meeting coverage will come from a future book-derived pass; treat wiki ENCOUNTERS as partial-by-design. Tier-3 (no qualifier). | Symmetric (character ↔ character) | — |

### Spatial & Temporal

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `LOCATED_AT` | Entity at location (with book/chapter timestamp). Covers both event-at-place (battle location) and person-at-place (witness location). Deprecated synonym `LOCATED_IN` was emitted by an early parser variant; normalize on read. | Entity → Location | Location, Seat |
| `SEAT_OF` | Primary location of a house or faction | Location → House/Faction | Seat, Seats |
| `TRAVELS_TO` | Movement from one location to another | Traveler → Destination (note origin) | — |
| `TRAVELS_WITH` | Co-presence in someone's company, on the road OR in attendance at court/event. Covers both journeying together (Arya/Gendry/Hot Pie; Sam+Gilly+Aemon to Oldtown; Dunk+Egg) AND retinue/court presence (Robett kneeling among Catelyn's welcomers at Winterfell). Distinct from `COMPANION_OF` (sustained friendship), `ALLIES_WITH` (political), `MEMBER_OF` (faction affiliation), `SERVES` (subordination). Tier-3 (no qualifier). | Symmetric (character ↔ character) | — |
| `BORN_AT` | Birthplace | Person → Location | Born |
| `DIED_AT` | Place of death | Person → Location | Died |
| `BURIED_AT` | Place of burial or interment | Person → Location | Buried |
| `IMPRISONED_AT` | Captive's place of confinement during a specific imprisonment (cell, dungeon, sky cell, black cells, tower used as gaol, prison hulk). Distinct from `LOCATED_AT` (general presence — not captivity-marked), `IMPRISONS` (jailer→captive judicial relationship), and `PRISONER_OF` (captive↔captor symmetric state). Examples: Ned/Black Cells; Tyrion/Eyrie sky cell; Jaime/Robb's camp at Riverrun; Davos/Dragonstone dungeon. Tier-3 (no qualifier). | Captive → Location (`place.location`) | — |
| `CONTEMPORARY_WITH` | Events happen simultaneously or overlap in time | Symmetric | — |
| `REGION_OF` | Location belongs to a larger geographic region | Location → Region | Region, Regions |

### Possession & Ownership

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `WIELDS` | Currently bears or uses a weapon/artifact. **Target MUST be `object.artifact`** — animals (mounts, ravens, dragons) use `OWNS` or `BONDED_TO`, never `WIELDS`. | Person → Artifact | — |
| `OWNS` | Possesses (broader than wields — castles, ships, animals) | Owner → Owned | Owner, Owners |
| `ANCESTRAL_WEAPON_OF` | Valyrian steel sword or other hereditary weapon of a house | Weapon → House | Ancestral weapon |
| `FORGED_BY` | Creator/smith of an artifact (the person/group who made it). **NOT for material composition** — use `MADE_OF` for substance/material relationships. | Creator → Artifact | — |
| `MADE_OF` | Artifact is composed of a material (Valyrian steel, dragonglass, dragonbone, weirwood, etc.). Distinct from `FORGED_BY` (smith). | Artifact → Material (`object.material`) | — |
| `LOOTED_BY` | Artifact taken by force or conquest from prior holder. Distinct from `OWNS` (steady state). Captures the transactional moment. | Artifact → New holder | — |
| `REFORGED_INTO` | Original artifact materially transformed into a new artifact (or multiple). The original ceases to exist; the new artifact(s) inherit material and lineage. | Original artifact → Resulting artifact | — |
| `GIFTED_TO` | Deliberate voluntary transfer of an artifact from one person to another as gift or honor. Distinct from `OWNS` (state) and `INHERITED_BY` (death-succession). Note giver in qualifier. | Artifact → Recipient | — |
| `INHERITED_BY` | Artifact passed via inheritance from deceased holder to heir. | Artifact → Heir | — |
| `WIELDED_IN` | Artifact was used in a named event (battle, execution, ritual). Distinct from `WIELDS` (person → artifact possession state). Enables artifact-history queries. | Artifact → Event | — |
| `EXECUTED_WITH` | A specific person was executed with a specific weapon (poetic-detail edges: Eddard executed with Ice, etc.). May overlap with `WIELDED_IN` + `EXECUTES`; kept distinct for narrative-precision queries. | Victim → Weapon | — |
| `PURCHASED_FROM` | Transactional acquisition of an artifact (or service) via purchase — distinct from `OWNS` (steady state), `GIFTED_TO` (voluntary transfer), `LOOTED_BY` (taken by force), `INHERITED_BY` (death-succession). Captures the transactional moment + the seller. Examples: Dunk purchasing the dragon-of-Pentos shield from Pate the Old; ship-passage purchases (when concretely named); merchant exchanges that are plot-significant. | Buyer → Seller | — |
| `BUILT` | Character physically built or oversaw the construction of a named structure (castle, tower, sept, wall, monument). Distinct from `FOUNDED` (scoped to organizations/houses/orders) and `OWNS` (steady state). Use when prose explicitly names the builder of a place. Examples: Brandon-the-Builder/The Wall, Brandon-the-Builder/Storm's End (legend), Lord-Triston-Hightower/Starry-Sept. | Builder → Structure (`place.location`) | — |
| `CAPTAIN_OF` | Character is captain (master/commander) of a named vessel. Target MUST be `object.artifact` (the vessel). Distinct from `COMMANDS` (military org) and `OWNS` (ownership state — captains may or may not own the vessel). Examples: Davos Seaworth/Black Betha, Victarion Greyjoy/Iron Victory, Asha Greyjoy/Black Wind, Salladhor Saan/Valyrian. | Captain → Vessel (`object.artifact`) | — |
| `CREW_OF` | Character serves as a crew member (non-captain) of a named vessel — sibling to `CAPTAIN_OF`. Target MUST be `object.artifact` (the vessel). Use when prose explicitly names a non-captain role (oarsman, first mate, ship's cook, sail-master). Captain → use `CAPTAIN_OF`. Generic faction membership → use `MEMBER_OF`. Note specific role in `notes` if known ("first mate"). | Crew member → Vessel (`object.artifact`) | — |

### Identity & Disguise

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `ALIAS_OF` | Known by another name. **Substitution test (canonical rule, S86 2026-06-08):** two strings are aliases iff substituting one for the other in any sentence about the entity does NOT change the truth value. "Wedding at the Twins" ↔ "Red Wedding" passes — same event. "Lord Walder calls for the bedding" ↔ "Red Wedding" FAILS — the bedding-call is a moment WITHIN the Red Wedding, not the whole event. Granularity differences are NOT aliases; emit `SUB_BEAT_OF` instead. The dashes-vs-spaces test catches surface variants only, NOT granularity. | Alias → True Identity | Alias, Aliases |
| `DISGUISED_AS` | Actively pretending to be someone/something else | Person → Disguise Identity | — |
| `SAME_AS` | Two references that resolve to the same entity (for cross-identity matching) | Symmetric | — |
| `IMPERSONATES` | Pretending to be a specific other person | Impersonator → Impersonated | — |

### Magic & Supernatural

> These edges capture relationships that involve magical, ritual, or supernatural agency — distinct from in-world physical relations. ASOIAF has rich magical systems (warging, R'hllor blood magic, Faceless-Men identity-transfer, weirwood-bonding, dragon-bonding, resurrection, curse-laying) that infobox extraction cannot reach. These types are *prose-derived only* — the wiki-infobox parser does not emit them.

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `WARGS_INTO` | A warg / skinchanger actively occupies the consciousness of an animal or person — e.g., Bran into Summer / Hodor / the heart tree; Arya into Nymeria (dream-skinchange); Varamyr into his wolf / eagle / shadowcat; Orell into his eagle; Jon (low-key) into Ghost | Warg → Vessel | — |
| `BONDED_TO` | Static magical bond between two beings — broader and more permanent than `WARGS_INTO` (which is the active occupation moment). Covers dragon-rider bonds (Daenerys ↔ Drogon, etc.), warg-animal lifelong pairing (Bran ↔ Summer when not actively warging), weirwood-bond (Bran ↔ his three-eyed-crow / weirwood network) | Symmetric | — |
| `SACRIFICES` | Deliberate ritual or magical killing with supernatural/symbolic purpose — distinct from `KILLS` (combat) and `EXECUTES` (judicial). Mirri Maz Duur sacrificing Drogo's life-essence; Daenerys sacrificing her unborn child to magic the dragon eggs; Stannis (via Melisandre) sacrificing Edric Storm's leech-blood / Mance / Penny's brother / (theory-tier) Shireen; Craster sacrificing his sons to the Others | Sacrificer → Victim | — |
| `RESURRECTS` | Returns the dead to life via supernatural means — distinct from `HEALS` (medical), distinct from `KILLED_BY` (semantic reverse). Thoros of Myr resurrects Beric Dondarrion (multiple times); Beric resurrects Catelyn → Lady Stoneheart (ASOS Epilogue); Coldhands resurrected by unknown force (Children?); Patchface drowned-and-returned; Qyburn reanimates the Mountain; Red Priests broadly perform this (Thoros, Moqorro) — `HEALS` is for body-restoration, `RESURRECTS` is for death-reversal | Resurrector → Resurrected | — |
| `CURSES` | A character or magical force lays a curse — Mirri Maz Duur's "when the sun rises in the west" curse on Daenerys; Maggy the Frog's Valonqar prophecy-curse on Cersei; the Curse of Harrenhal (collective); Night's King lore | Curser → Cursed | — |
| `PRACTICES` | Character actively practices a named magical or ritual discipline. Target is `concept.magic` (or `concept.craft` for non-magical-but-named-discipline cases). Distinct from `WARGS_INTO` (active occupation moment), `BONDED_TO` (static pairing), `WORSHIPS` (religious devotion, not the magical practice itself), `CLERGY_OF` (religious office). Examples: Melisandre/shadow-binding, Mirri Maz Duur/maegi-blood-magic, Bran/greendreams, faceless-men/identity-transfer, Qyburn/necromancy. | Character → Magic discipline | — |

### Cultural & Religious

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `CULTURE_OF` | Person belongs to a cultural group | Person → Culture | Culture, Race |
| `WORSHIPS` | Follows or serves a deity/religion | Person → Religion | Religion |
| `SACRED_TO` | Location or artifact is holy to a religion | Entity → Religion | — |
| `CLERGY_OF` | Serves as religious official | Person → Religion | — |
| `OFFICIATES` | Character performs the ritual / religious / ceremonial role at a named event (weddings, funerals, coronations, kingsmoots, namedays, knighting ceremonies). Distinct from `CLERGY_OF` (general clergy status, target = religion) and `ATTENDS` (guest/audience). Target is `event.*` or specific named ceremony node. Examples: Melisandre/wedding-of-sigorn-and-alys-karstark; the High Septon/coronation-of-tommen-i; Aeron Damphair/kingsmoot-of-299-ac. | Character → Event | — |
| `HONORED_AT` | Character is the **ceremonial honoree** — the recipient of a conferred honor at a named event (queen of love and beauty crowned at a tourney, a nameday honoree, the dubbed knight at a knighting). The reification-role complement on a ceremonial event hub: the **conferrer** uses `AGENT_IN` (e.g. Rhaegar laying the laurel), the **honoree** uses `HONORED_AT`. Distinct from `ATTENDS` (audience), `OFFICIATES` (performs the rite), `WITNESS_IN` (perceiver), `AGENT_IN` (the one who acts/confers). Added Session 134 (2026-06-23) to retire the off-vocab `CROWNS_QUEEN_OF_LOVE_AND_BEAUTY` tail-LLM leakage; first instance `lyanna-stark HONORED_AT crowning-of-lyanna-at-harrenhal`. Tier-1 when verbatim-cited; no qualifier. | Character → Event (`event.*`) | — |
| `NAMED_AFTER` | An entity was given its name in honor of, or as a reference to, another entity. Captures Westeros's pervasive dynastic name-recycling culture (Rickard Karstark named for Rickard Stark, the many Brandons and Aegons). Distinct from `ALIAS_OF` (a different name for the same entity), `SAME_AS` (identity-resolution), and `DEPICTED_IN` (in-world legend about a person). One-sided: the named entity points to its namesake. Surfaced in Sonnet `no-fitting-type` rejections. Tier-3 (no qualifier). | Entity → Namesake-entity | wiki (dynastic naming patterns) |

### Narrative & Literary

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `DEPICTED_IN` | Character is the subject of an in-world text/song/ballad/tale (Danny Flint → "Brave Danny Flint" song, Florian → Florian-and-Jonquil tales). Distinct from `WRITTEN_BY` (author → work). Captures the in-universe legacy/folklore layer. | Character → Text | — |

*continued:*

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `FORESHADOWS` | Detail A is a Chekhov's gun for Event B | Detail → Event | — |
| `PARALLELS` | Event/character A mirrors Event/character B thematically | Symmetric | — |
| `SUBVERTS` | Event A inverts the expectation set by B | Subverter → Subverted | — |
| `ECHOES` | Weaker than PARALLELS — structural or verbal similarity without full thematic mirroring | Echo → Source | — |
| `CONTRASTS` | Deliberate opposition in characterization, situation, or outcome | Symmetric | — |
| `WRITTEN_BY` | Authorship of an in-world text (book, song, decree, letter) | Text → Author | Written by |

### Prophecy

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `FULFILLS` | Event fulfills prophecy (confirmed) | Event → Prophecy | — |
| `APPEARS_TO_FULFILL` | Possible fulfillment, may be red herring | Event → Prophecy | — |
| `SUBVERTS_PROPHECY` | Contradicts expected fulfillment | Event → Prophecy | — |
| `PROPHESIED_BY` | Who made the prophecy | Prophecy → Prophet | — |
| `SUBJECT_OF_PROPHECY` | Person/event the prophecy is about | Person → Prophecy | — |
| `DREAMS_OF` | In-world prophetic or significant dream/vision about a person, place, event, or symbol. Distinct from `FORESHADOWS` (which is a reader-facing narrative-craft edge); `DREAMS_OF` is character-facing. Heavily concentrated in Bran (greendreams, three-eyed-crow), Daenerys (HotU visions), Jojen, and the Targaryen line (canonical prophetic-dreaming lineage) | Dreamer → Subject | — |

### Evidentiary (Theory Support)

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `SUPPORTS` | A real passage the theory community reads as evidence FOR a theory — the edge asserts the reading exists, not that the theory is true. Every edge carries its own byte-verified verbatim quote + chapter:line ref | Evidence node → Theory | — |
| `CONTRADICTS` | A real passage that undermines a theory (or one scoped sub-claim — scope lives in the edge `note`) | Evidence node → Theory | — |
| `CITED_BY` | DEFERRED (S216) — no out-of-world source node type; provenance lives in theory-node frontmatter (`video_sources`, `origin`) | Theory → Source | — |

**Theory-layer conventions (ratified S216–S217, Matt):**

- **Tier floor is absolute:** theory nodes and SUPPORTS/CONTRADICTS edges are **tier 3–5 ALWAYS** — no "event-anchored" or other exceptions. Per-edge tier variance under a node is allowed (a tier-3 node may carry tier-4/5 edges).
- **Layer rule:** facts already owned by tier-1/2 nodes are the theory's *premise* (prose), not theory evidence — don't re-mint them as edges. An existing node CAN source an edge where its confirmed reality bears directly on the claim (the subject-link pattern).
- **Subject-link pattern:** a theory may link to the event/artifact node it is *about* via a scoped evidence edge (GNC G1 precedent: the confirmed event node sources a SUPPORTS into the theory that generalizes it).
- **Parallel-FORESHADOWS (S217):** when a theory-evidence quote foreshadows an event that actually occurs on the page, the factual layer gets a `FORESHADOWS` edge to that event node (canon-visible; tier 2–4 by mapping strength, typically 2–3) alongside the theory-layer SUPPORTS edge. Both edges may carry the same quote — different layers, different claims.
- **Naming:** display names are claim-style ("Roose Bolton is a skin-stealing immortal"); genuinely unresolved multi-candidate whodunits use question-form ("Who is Coldhands?", "Who Wrote the Pink Letter?", "Who is the Hooded Man of Winterfell?").
- **One node per canonical named theory;** umbrella wiki "X/Theories" stubs stay dark unless they ARE the canonical theory.
- **Evidence sources:** any node class whose entity the quote is about may source an evidence edge — including `object.food` (weirwood-paste), legend/`object.text` (rat-cook), and `concept` nodes (wights). From wave 2 onward, theory edges may cite `fab-*` (Fire & Blood) chapters — same quotecheck machinery; chronicler unreliability handled by tier + `in_universe_source`.
- **Ungrounded material** (show-only, TWOW/TWOIAF-fenced, GRRM interviews, community reasoning) is domain-labelled in the node body — never edges.
- **Chat exposure:** theories are invisible to the chat persona (SHARED_RULES guardrail). Plan of record (S217): an off-by-default UI toggle — OFF = runtime tool-layer filter strips theory nodes/edges; ON = labeled-speculation prompt contract. Build is a separate gated session.

### Causal & Plot

| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| `CAUSES` | Event A leads to Event B | Cause → Effect | — |
| `PREVENTS` | Action A blocks Event B | Preventer → Prevented | — |
| `ENABLES` | Condition A makes Event B possible | Enabler → Enabled | — |
| `MOTIVATES` | Event or condition drives a character's actions | Motivation → Actor | — |
| `TRIGGERS` | Immediate cause (narrower than CAUSES — the specific spark) | Trigger → Result | — |
| `SUSPECTED_OF` | A character is suspected — in-world and/or by the reader — of being the agent or cause of an event, where the published text does NOT prove it. The unproven counterpart to `AGENT_IN`/`KILLS`/`CAUSES`: use when the suspicion is itself load-bearing but the act is unconfirmed in the 5 published books. **Capped at Tier-2 — NEVER Tier-1** (Tier-1 is reserved for proven canon; an asserted-as-fact killing uses `KILLS`/`AGENT_IN`). Cite the in-text basis (accusation, portents, timing) in the rationale; out-of-text corroboration (TWOW-preview, author statements) may be noted but does not raise the tier. Added Session 116 (2026-06-20) — first instance: `euron-greyjoy SUSPECTED_OF death-of-balon-greyjoy` (Asha's accusation "Silence returns within a day of my lord father's death" + the storm-portents; Euron's confession exists only in the *Forsaken* TWOW-preview chapter, not published canon, so the killing stays unproven and the node stays `event.death`, not `event.assassination`). | Suspect → Event (`event.*`) | — |

#### The `ENABLES` vs `CAUSES` vs `TRIGGERS` contract (S121, 2026-06-21)

The three forward-causal edges are NOT interchangeable; they form a strength ladder, and the choice is load-bearing for graph honesty (the "sequence-only trap" — drawing causation where only chronology exists). Pick by this rubric:

- **`TRIGGERS`** — A is the **immediate spark** of B; B is the very next beat, nothing decisional sits between them. *Is B the very next thing, or is there a step in between?* If next → TRIGGERS. (`ned-confesses-to-treason TRIGGERS execution-of-eddard-stark`.)

content.content:
1	# TSS — Dunk 1
2	
3	## Chapter Metadata
4	- **book:** TSS
5	- **chapter_number:** 1
6	- **pov_character:** Dunk
7	- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
8	- **first_available:** pre-agot
9	- **location_primary:** Standfast (the tower — undercellar, roof, and Ser Eustace's solar)
10	- **location_secondary:** Coldmoat (destination/referenced); Dorne (dream setting — red mountains, white sands); Ashford Meadow (recalled)
11	- **approximate_timeline:** Within this unit: one night (a cellar bath, then a nightmare on the roof), the following morning (a failed shield-wall drill, then a meeting in Ser Eustace's solar), and that evening (a second bath and planning). Ends with Dunk resolved to ride to Coldmoat "on the morrow."
12	- **time_markers:** "tonight"; "as the last kettle was coming to a boil"; "a good year and a half" (Egg's service to Dunk); "twenty" days (Dunk's hyperbole for Egg's service); "Come the break of day"; "The morning was already hot"; "upon the morrow" / "On the morrow"; "the next time the septon comes" (Will Barleycorn's wedding); "a fortnight" / "longer than a fortnight" (Osgrey sons' training of the levy); "five-and-twenty years" (Daeron II's reign); "nine-and-thirty" (Baelor's age at death); "a moon's turn"; "for a thousand years before the Conquest" (Osgreys as Marshalls); "one winter day" (Eustace's marriage proposal to Lord Wyman); "In a year, his bones will be scoured clean"; "two years as a page at King's Landing" (Egg); "for hundreds of years" (Targaryen sibling-marriage)
13	- **chapter_summary:** Dunk bathes in Standfast's undercellar with Egg's help while the two argue about the peasant levy Ser Eustace means to arm against the Widow's men; Dunk reflects on Egg's concealed identity as Aegon Targaryen. Sleeping on the roof, Dunk suffers a nightmare of digging graves in the Dornish sands for his dead horse Chestnut, his dead masters, and the doomed recruits. At dawn a shield-wall drill fails when the recruits break and flee before Dunk's charging warhorse, and Dunk reports to Ser Eustace that the levy will not serve. Eustace proposes paying Lady Webber a "blood price" to take down her dam and recounts the fallen history of House Osgrey and his vow never to enter Coldmoat, after which Dunk agrees to ride to Coldmoat himself to treat with the Red Widow. That evening Dunk takes a second bath while Egg relays rumors of the Red Widow's poisonings and black arts, and Dunk forbids Egg from accompanying him.
14	- **unit_part:** 3 of 8
15	
16	## Physical Environment
17	- **Weather:** Severe drought; "The next time it rained on them up there would be the first." Savage heat by day.
18	- **Season indicators:** High summer / prolonged dry spell; "After the savage heat of the day"; "The morning was already hot"; a night sky thick with stars.
19	- **Time of day:** Night (first bath, then stargazing and the nightmare on the roof); dawn ("the break of day") for the drill; morning for the solar meeting; evening/night for the second bath.
20	- **Lighting:** The cellar is dim and lamplit ("In the dimness of the lamplit cellar"); Egg's eyes look black in the dimness but deep dark purple in better light. The solar is dim ("in the dimness of his solar"). Night roof lit by thousands of stars.
21	- **Sounds:** Splashing of bathwater; the empty kettle thumping onto the earthen floor; hens screeching and flapping under the warhorse; woven branches cracking and splintering under iron-shod hooves; Ser Bennis rattling off curses; a fat black fly buzzing.
22	- **Smells:** Ser Bennis "smelled like a bad cheese" (lice and fleas); Dunk fears turning up before Lady Webber "smelling like Ser Bennis"; Egg jokes of "a tub of Maester's droppings"; Dunk's sweat-stained tunic.
23	- **Notable sensory details:** Steam rising "in a white curtain" as hot water is poured; scalding bathwater cooler farther down; dirt coming off Dunk's arms "in long gray clouds"; water gone "gray and cold"; the dream's "dry hot earth" and "fine sand"; red froth bubbling on Bennis's lips; the sands filling Dunk's mouth, nose, and eyes.
24	
25	## Characters Present
26	| Character | Role in Chapter | First Appearance? | Notes |
27	|-----------|----------------|-------------------|-------|
28	| Ser Duncan the Tall (Dunk) | POV; bathes, drills the levy, meets Ser Eustace, agrees to go to Coldmoat | No | "Dunk of Flea Bottom"; a hedge knight, now Ser Eustace's sworn sword |
29	| Egg | Dunk's squire; draws bathwater, argues over the levy, relays rumors | No | Revealed in-text as Aegon Targaryen |
30	| Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield | Drills the recruits in a shield wall at dawn; also appears in Dunk's nightmare | No | "crawls with lice and fleas and smelled like a bad cheese" |
31	| Ser Eustace Osgrey | Dunk's liege; meets him in the solar, proposes the blood price, recounts Osgrey history | No | A landed knight; the Osgreys were once Marshalls of the Northmarch |
32	| Big Rob | Recruit; "the first to drop his spear and run" in the drill; also a corpse in the nightmare | No | A simpleton; "doesn't even know his left foot from his right" |
33	| The eight recruits (Standfast levy) | Lined up shoulder to shoulder for the shield-wall drill; break and flee | No | Includes Wet Wat and Will Barleycorn (named); shields are woven wood |
34	| Ser Arlan of Pennytree | Dream apparition; reproaches Dunk for never weeping for him | No | Dunk's dead former master; died of a chill (dream/recalled) |
35	| Baelor Breakspear | Dream apparition; speaks from the grave | No | Dead prince; "the hope of Westeros" (dream/recalled) |
36	| Prince Valarr | Dream apparition; asks why the gods took his father and left Dunk | No | "the Young Prince," Baelor's son (dream/recalled) |
37	| Treb | Dream apparition; standing "with an arrow in his eye" | Yes (uncertain — verify) | Appears only as a corpse in the nightmare |
38	| Wet Wat | Dream apparition (corpse, head "cut near in half"); also named among the living recruits | No | A living recruit, "still half a boy"; dead only in the dream |
39	| Lem | Dream apparition ("old Lem"); a living Osgrey war-survivor | No | Came back from the war with Eustace; dead only in the dream |
40	| Pate | Dream apparition ("red-eyed Pate"); a living Osgrey war-survivor | No | Came back from the war with Eustace; dead only in the dream |
41	
42	## Character Appearances
43	| Character | Hair | Eyes | Build/Height | Scars/Marks | Clothing/Armor | Weapons Worn | Distinguishing Features | Age (if stated) |
44	|-----------|------|------|-------------|-------------|----------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------|
45	| Egg | Shaven head (kept shaved or dyed); true hair "shone like beaten gold and strands of silver woven all together" | "deep and dark and purple" — Valyrian eyes; look black in dim light; "big eyes," made larger by the shaven head | A boy; "half a boy" is said of Wat, not Egg | Two hairs growing on his cheek, below the ear | — | — | Blushes "redder than a pomegranate"; shaven head rubbed for luck by orphan girls | Not stated; youngest of Maekar's four sons; served Dunk ~1½ years |
46	| Ser Duncan the Tall (Dunk) | Filthy hair (this night); once had "some fuzz growing on his upper lip" as a youth | — | Very tall and large; "It had been years since Dunk had met anyone as tall as he was"; "size and strength could make up for many lacks" | — | Sweat-stained tunic; a sandsilk "Dornish tunic" painted with his elm and falling star (his finest); chainmail worn all day | Longsword (kept sharp by Egg) | Poor with words and "worse with women" | — |
47	| Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield | — | — | — | — | Carries a brown shield (by his epithet) | — | "crawls with lice and fleas and smelled like a bad cheese"; chews sourleaf; red froth on his lips (dream) | — |
48	| Ser Eustace Osgrey | — | — | — | — | "my richest raiment" (recalled, for the marriage proposal) | — | Has a mustache that "quivered"; rubs his lip beneath it; an old knight | — |
49	| Prince Valarr (dream apparition) | "his father's light brown hair, but a streak of silver-gold ran through it" | "cool blue eyes" | — | — | — | — | "the Young Prince" | — |
50	| Baelor Breakspear (dream apparition) | — | — | — | — | — | — | "his prince, the hope of Westeros" | "only nine-and-thirty" at death |
51	
52	## Characters Referenced
53	| Character | Context of Reference | Referenced By |
54	|-----------|---------------------|---------------|
55	| Aegon Targaryen | Egg's true name and identity; "went back to Summerhall with his brother Daeron after the tourney at Ashford Meadow" (the cover story) | Narration / Dunk |
56	| Maekar, Prince of Summerhall | Egg's father; fourth son of Daeron II; sent Egg to squire in secret; dealt the blow that killed Baelor (as "his brother") | Narration / Egg / Dunk |
57	| King Daeron the Good, the Second of His Name (Daeron II) | Egg's grandfather; sat the Iron Throne five-and-twenty years until the Great Spring Sickness took him | Narration |
58	| Daeron (Egg's brother) | Supposedly returned to Summerhall with Aegon after the Ashford tourney (the cover story) | Dunk |
59	| Lord Bloodraven | "How many eyes does Lord Bloodraven have? A thousand eyes, and one."; Shiera is his paramour | Dunk (internal) / Egg |
60	| Tanselle (Too-Tall) | Painted the falling star on Dunk's shield at Ashford; the "puppet girl" Dunk rescued from Aerion; "not too tall for me" | Dunk (internal) / Egg |
61	| Roger of Pennytree | "younger than Wet Wat when he died on the Redgrass Field" | Dunk |
62	| Sam Stoops | Told Dunk that Coldmoat was once the Osgreys'; reports Coldmoat's castellan is "as big as you are" | Dunk / Egg |
63	| Sam Stoops' wife | Soaks and beats the laundry; says the Red Widow "sold her babes unborn to the Lord of the Seven Hells" | Narration / Egg |
64	| Will Barleycorn | Recruit "to be married the next time the septon comes" | Egg |
65	| Lady Vaith | "was mad"; Dunk's words to her nearly got them killed in Dorne; "the only one you ever knew" | Egg / Dunk |
66	| Aerion | Dunk rescued the puppet girl from him; "Aerion's in Lys" | Egg / Dunk |
67	| Ser Lucas Inchfield (the Longinch) | Castellan of Coldmoat; "called the Longinch for his size"; Lord Wyman laughed with him at Eustace's proposal; informed Eustace of Dake's fate | Egg / Ser Eustace |
68	| Lady Webber (the Red Widow) | Dammed the Chequy Water; reputed to have poisoned all four husbands, borne no living children, and dealt in black arts; carried off Dake | Egg / Ser Eustace |
69	| Lord Perwyn Osgrey (Perwyn the Proud) | Raised Coldmoat, "the greatest of our seats" | Ser Eustace |
70	| King Maegor | "Aegon's son King Maegor who took Coldmoat from us" after Ormond Osgrey opposed him | Ser Eustace |
71	| Aegon the Dragon | "the greatest [king] since Aegon the Dragon" | Prince Valarr (dream) |
72	| Lord Ormond Osgrey | Spoke out against Maegor's suppression of the Stars and Swords; lost Coldmoat | Ser Eustace |
73	| Reynard Webber | "old Reynard Webber," on whom Eustace's father once called at Coldmoat | Ser Eustace |
74	| Addam Osgrey | Eustace's son; served at Coldmoat as page and squire; a "fondness grew up between him and Lord Wyman's daughter" | Ser Eustace |
75	| Lord Wyman Webber | Refused Eustace's marriage proposal for Addam; laughed with Lucas Inchfield | Ser Eustace |
76	| Lord Wyman's daughter | Object of Addam's fondness and Eustace's rejected marriage proposal | Ser Eustace |
77	| Alysanne Osgrey | Eustace's dead daughter; "You are the sort of man I had always hoped that she might marry" | Ser Eustace / Egg |
78	| Lord Rowan | Eustace expects no justice from him | Ser Eustace |
79	| "this king" (unnamed) | Eustace expects no justice from him | Ser Eustace |
80	| Lady Shiera | "Lord Bloodraven's paramour"; "bathes in blood to keep her beauty" | Egg |
81	| Rhae | Egg's sister; "put a love potion in my drink, so I'd marry her" | Egg |
82	| Daella | Egg's sister; Rhae wanted Egg to marry her instead | Egg |
83	| Dake | Osgrey forager and war-survivor; carried off by the Widow's people and found "at the bottom of the moat" at Coldmoat | Ser Eustace / Dunk |
84	
85	## Locations
86	| Location | Role | First Appearance? |
87	|----------|------|-------------------|
88	| Standfast | Ser Eustace's tower; Dunk's and Egg's home; undercellar bath, rooftop sleeping, and the solar meeting | No |
89	| Coldmoat | The Red Widow's castle; Dunk's destination; once the greatest Osgrey seat | No |
90	| The Chequy Water | The stream the Red Widow has dammed | No |
91	| The dam | The Widow's dam blocking the Chequy Water | No |
92	| Dorne | Setting of Dunk's nightmare and recalled crossing; red mountains, white sands | No |
93	| Prince's Pass | Recalled: Dunk and Egg climbed it; near where Chestnut died | No |
94	| Vaith | Recalled: Chestnut died on the crossing to Vaith | No |
95	| The Greenblood | Recalled river; poled down it by poleboat, orphan girls rubbed Egg's head | No |
96	| Planky Town | Recalled: took passage for Oldtown; Dunk drank too much there | No |
97	| Oldtown | Recalled destination of the White Lady passage | No |
98	| Summerhall | Maekar's seat; the cover story says Aegon returned there | No |
99	| Ashford Meadow | Recalled tourney; falling star; Dunk gained Egg and nearly lost a hand and foot | No |
100	| Redgrass Field | Recalled battle where Roger of Pennytree died | No |
101	| King's Landing | Where Egg served two years as a page | No |
102	| Lys | Where Aerion now is | No |
103	| Flea Bottom | Dunk's origin; "I'm Dunk of Flea Bottom" | No |
104	| Field of Fire | After it, "Highgarden passed from kings to stewards" and the Osgreys dwindled | No |
105	| Highgarden | "passed from kings to stewards" after the Field of Fire | No |
106	| The Northmarch | The Osgreys were its Marshalls for a thousand years before the Conquest | No |
107	| The Seven Hells | "the Lord of the Seven Hells," to whom the Widow supposedly sold her babes | No |
108	
109	## Location Descriptions
110	| Location | Defensive Features | Architecture/Layout | Interior Details | Scale | Condition | Surrounding Terrain/Geography | Notable Sensory Details |
111	|----------|--------------------|--------------------|-----------------| ------|-----------|------------------------------|------------------------|
112	| Standfast | A tower ("rode Thunder back to the tower") | Undercellar with a well; a solar reached by steps; a flat roof used for sleeping and drying laundry | Undercellar is "a dank chamber walled in stone and earth"; a big stone washtub, a hearth, a big iron kettle; thick straw pallets; the solar holds the Little Lion's shield | Small (a hedge-knight-scale holding) | Humble; a drought means the roof never sees rain | Open country near the Chequy Water; hens and chickens about the base | Dim, lamplit cellar; steam; a fat black fly in the solar |
113	| Coldmoat | A moat; a drawbridge; gates | "a chequy lion carved into the stone above the gates"; a high seat within | — | "the greatest of our seats" (of the old Osgrey castles) | Held by House Webber; castellan Lucas Inchfield | — | — |
114	| Dorne (dream/recalled) | — | — | — | Vast "deep sands" and "waste" | Arid; "Water is precious in the waste" | Red mountains in the distance, white sands underfoot; snakes, scorpions, sand dogs; merchants with mules, wayns, and sand sledges | Dry hot earth; fine sand that "flowed back into the grave as fast as they could fling it out" |
115	
116	## Artifacts & Objects of Significance
117	| Artifact | Context | First Appearance? | Current Holder/Location |
118	|----------|---------|-------------------|------------------------|
119	| The Little Lion's shield | Hangs in Ser Eustace's solar; he runs his fingers across it as he speaks of Osgrey history | No | Ser Eustace's solar, Standfast |
120	| Dunk's shield (elm and falling star) | Bears an elm and a falling star; Tanselle painted the star at Ashford | No | Dunk |
121	| Dunk's Dornish tunic | Sandsilk, "the finest garment that he owned," painted with his elm and falling star; to be worn at Coldmoat | Yes (uncertain — verify) | Dunk |
122	| Egg's boot | The concealed token Egg keeps urging they use for aid; "the boot is only meant for dire need" | No | Egg |

125	| Chestnut | Dunk's old "swaybacked stot"; bore him for years, "never bucked or bit"; died of thirst on the crossing to Vaith; buried in the nightmare | No | Dead (Dorne) |

130	
131	## Food & Drink
132	| Meal/Occasion | Food Items Described | Drink | Who Is Eating/Drinking | Where | Preparation/Presentation Notes |
133	|--------------|---------------------|-------|----------------------|-------|-------------------------------|

135	| Sourleaf (chewed) | Sourleaf (a chewing leaf that stains the mouth red) | — | Ser Bennis and the dead men (in the dream) | Dream / general habit | "They had all been chewing sourleaf with Bennis... but then he realized that it was blood trickling from their mouths" |
136	
137	## Hospitality & Guest Right
138	| Event | Type | Host | Guest(s) | Details |
139	|-------|------|------|----------|---------|
140	| Dunk's planned visit to Coldmoat | Warning against taking hospitality | Lady Webber (the Red Widow) | Ser Duncan (planned) | Egg warns: "You'd best not take any food or drink at Coldmoat, ser. The Red Widow poisoned all her husbands." |
141	| Dunk lodged at Standfast | Service/shelter | Ser Eustace Osgrey | Ser Duncan and Egg | Dunk and Egg given straw pallets in the cellar; Dunk is Eustace's sworn sword |
142	
143	## Events & Actions
144	1. **The cellar bath** — Dunk bathes in the stone washtub while Egg draws, heats, and pours the water; they argue over the doomed levy and Egg's suppressed identity.
145	   - Agent: Ser Duncan (Dunk)
146	   - Patient: —
147	   - Instrument: washtub, well bucket, iron kettle, soap, long-handled horsehair scrub brush
148	   - Location: Standfast undercellar
149	   - Outcome: Dunk bathed; Egg sent to sleep
150	2. **Egg presses to "use the boot"** — Egg repeatedly urges deploying his concealed token to summon aid for the recruits; Dunk flatly refuses ("No.") each time.
151	   - Agent: Egg
152	   - Patient: Ser Duncan
153	   - Location: Standfast undercellar
154	   - Outcome: Refused; "the boot is only meant for dire need"
155	3. **Dunk recalls Egg's true identity** — Dunk reflects that his squire is Aegon Targaryen, youngest son of Maekar, and that Maekar bade Egg keep his head shaved or dyed and tell no man his true name.
156	   - Agent: Ser Duncan (internal)
157	   - Patient: Egg
158	   - Location: Standfast undercellar
159	   - Outcome: Identity established for the reader
160	4. **The nightmare on the roof** — Dunk dreams he is digging a grave in the Dornish sands for his dead horse Chestnut while apparitions of Ser Arlan, Baelor Breakspear, and Prince Valarr reproach him; then Big Rob, Ser Bennis, Treb, Wet Wat, Lem, and Pate appear as gutted corpses, and Bennis tells Dunk to dig "eight for them and one for me and one for old Ser Useless, and one last one for your bald-head boy"; the sands collapse and bury Egg and then Dunk.
161	   - Agent: Ser Duncan (dreamer)
162	   - Patient: Egg (buried in the dream); the recruits (dead in the dream)
163	   - Location: Standfast roof / Dornish sands (dream)
164	   - Outcome: Dunk wakes troubled
165	5. **The shield-wall drill fails** — At dawn Ser Bennis lines the eight recruits shoulder to shoulder with shields touching and spears out; Dunk and Egg charge on horseback; Egg's mount Maester balks, but Thunder charges home; Big Rob drops his spear and flees first and the rest follow, and Thunder tramples their discarded woven shields.
166	   - Agent: Ser Duncan (and Ser Bennis, drillmaster)
167	   - Patient: The eight recruits
168	   - Instrument: Thunder (warhorse); the recruits' woven-wood shields and spears
169	   - Location: Outside Standfast
170	   - Instigator: Ser Bennis (set the drill); Ser Eustace (whose war it is)
171	   - Witness: Egg ("Egg fought manfully to hold his laughter in, but finally lost the battle")
172	   - Outcome: Drill collapses; recruits scatter
173	6. **Dunk reports the failure to Ser Eustace** — In the solar Dunk tells Eustace the levy "will not serve"; Eustace defends them, recalling that only three of his men (Lem, Pate, Dake) came home from the war.
174	   - Agent: Ser Duncan
175	   - Patient: Ser Eustace Osgrey
176	   - Location: Ser Eustace's solar
177	   - Outcome: Eustace concedes it "may take longer than a fortnight"
178	7. **Ser Eustace proposes the blood price and tells the Osgrey history** — Eustace proposes paying Lady Webber a silver stag for the wounded man and three for the insult if she takes down the dam; he recounts the Osgreys as former Marshalls of the Northmarch, the loss of Coldmoat to King Maegor, Addam's rejected suit for Lord Wyman's daughter, and the drowning of Dake, and reveals his vow never to enter Coldmoat again "unless to take possession."
179	   - Agent: Ser Eustace Osgrey
180	   - Patient: Ser Duncan
181	   - Location: Ser Eustace's solar
182	   - Outcome: The blood-price plan set; Eustace cannot go himself
183	8. **Dunk agrees to ride to Coldmoat** — Dunk volunteers ("I swore no vows") to carry the offer to the Red Widow; Eustace praises him as "the true steel" and the man he had hoped his daughter Alysanne might wed; Dunk kneels and agrees to go "upon the morrow."
184	   - Agent: Ser Duncan
185	   - Patient: Ser Eustace Osgrey
186	   - Location: Ser Eustace's solar
187	   - Outcome: Dunk committed to the Coldmoat mission
188	9. **The second bath and the Red Widow rumors** — That evening Dunk bathes again to be presentable; Egg relays gossip that the Red Widow poisoned all four husbands, bore no living children, and deals in black arts, and jokes about gallantry, songs, and the Kingsguard; Dunk chooses his sandsilk Dornish tunic for the visit.
189	   - Agent: Ser Duncan / Egg
190	   - Location: Standfast undercellar
191	   - Outcome: Preparations made
192	10. **Dunk forbids Egg to come to Coldmoat** — Egg argues a knight is more impressive with a squire; Dunk, fearing the danger (recalling the two men in crow cages), orders Egg to stay and help Bennis with the smallfolk.
193	    - Agent: Ser Duncan
194	    - Patient: Egg
195	    - Location: Standfast undercellar
196	    - Outcome: "You're not going, and that's the end of it."
197	
198	## Spatial Layout & Movement
199	| Phase | Who | Position / Movement | Relative To | Notes |
200	|-------|-----|---------------------|-------------|-------|
201	| Assembly | The eight recruits | Lined "shoulder to shoulder, with their shields touching and their spear points poking through" | Forming a shield wall | Bennis's dawn drill |
202	| Confrontation | Dunk (on Thunder) and Egg (on Maester) | Mounted and charged the shield wall | Toward the recruits | Maester balked ~ten feet from the spears; Thunder charged home |
203	| Dispersal | The recruits, led by Big Rob | Dropped spears and fled, leaving a gap; the rest joined the flight | Away from the charging horse | Thunder trampled their discarded shields |
204	| Arrival | Dunk | Rode Thunder back to the tower and dismounted; strode up the steps to the solar | To Ser Eustace | Handed his helm to Egg |

207	## Information Revealed

217	| Ser Arlan of Pennytree died of a chill; Baelor Breakspear died of a blow his brother (Maekar) dealt during Dunk's trial of seven; Prince Valarr died in the Great Spring Sickness | Dunk's dream-recollection | Dunk | Revealed within this unit |
218	| Coldmoat was once the Osgreys'; they were Marshalls of the Northmarch for a thousand years before the Conquest, with four castles; Coldmoat, their greatest seat, was raised by Perwyn the Proud | Ser Eustace (Sam Stoops had told Dunk the castle was once theirs) | Ser Eustace, Dunk, Sam Stoops | Revealed within this unit |
219	| King Maegor took Coldmoat from the Osgreys after Lord Ormond Osgrey spoke against his suppression of the Stars and Swords (the Poor Fellows and the Warrior's Sons) | Ser Eustace | Ser Eustace, Dunk | Revealed within this unit |
220	| Addam Osgrey and Lord Wyman's daughter grew fond; Eustace's marriage proposal was refused, and he heard Wyman laughing with Lucas Inchfield | Ser Eustace | Ser Eustace, Dunk | Revealed within this unit |

234	| Dunk | Egg | "So is this. The same thing, only smaller." | Answering Egg |
235	| Egg | Dunk | "Smaller and stupider, ser." | Retort |

237	| Egg | Dunk | "To keep my hair shaved or dyed, and tell no man my true name" | Repeating his father's charge, "with obvious reluctance" |
238	| Dunk | (internal) | "How many eyes does Lord Bloodraven have? A thousand eyes, and one." | While reminding Egg to guard his identity |
239	| Ser Bennis | Dunk (dream) | "Eight for them and one for me and one for old Ser Useless, and one last one for your bald-head boy." | Naming the graves to dig in the nightmare |
240	| Dunk | Ser Eustace | "No, m'lord... They will not serve." | Reporting the failed drill |
241	| Ser Eustace | Dunk | "It comes to me that in days gone by, when the green kings ruled, you could pay a man a blood price if you had slain one of his animals or peasants." | Proposing the blood price |
242	| Ser Eustace | Dunk | "I swore a holy vow that I would never set foot inside that castle again, unless to take possession." | Explaining why he cannot go to Coldmoat |
243	| Ser Eustace | Dunk | "You are the sort of man I had always hoped that she might marry. A true knight, Ser Duncan." | Of his dead daughter Alysanne |
244	| Dunk | Ser Eustace | "I could go, m'lord. I swore no vows." | Volunteering for Coldmoat |
245	| Egg | Dunk | "You'd best not take any food or drink at Coldmoat, ser. The Red Widow poisoned all her husbands." | Warning about the Red Widow |
246	| Egg | Dunk | "I want to be a knight of the Kingsguard, and live only to serve and defend the king. The Kingsguard are sworn not to wed." | On his ambition |
247	| Dunk | Egg | "I'm not like to marry her. She's a highborn lady, and I'm Dunk of Flea Bottom, remember?" | On the Red Widow |
248	
249	## POV Character's Internal State
250	- **Emotional state:** Weary and grief-haunted (the nightmare of dead masters, his dead horse, and the doomed recruits leaves him "soiled and sticky as if he'd never bathed"); affectionate toward Egg ("almost as a little brother"); apprehensive about the Coldmoat mission and daunted more by "the prospect of facing the Red Widow" than by the giant Lucas Longinch; quietly flattered and reddening at Eustace's praise and talk of Alysanne.
251	- **Primary preoccupation:** The coming confrontation over the dam — how to treat with Lady Webber without begging, how to keep the doomed recruits and Egg from being killed, and rehearsing what to say to a highborn lady.
252	- **Key decisions made:** Refuses to let Egg "use the boot"; agrees to ride to Coldmoat to offer the blood price and ask for the dam's removal; will wear his sandsilk Dornish tunic; forbids Egg to accompany him and orders him to stay and help Bennis with the smallfolk.
253	- **Self-deception flags:** Tells the recruits' deaths are "their duty... to die, if need be" and "The same thing, only smaller" while privately thinking "this is madness" and "they will soon be dead lads, if they go up against the knights of Coldmoat." Insists to himself of the dream, "It never happened that way... I never wept," while admitting "I might have wanted to." Downplays his dread of the Red Widow as "I'm going to talk to the Red Widow, that's all."
254

# TSS — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** TSS
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Standfast (the tower — undercellar, roof, and Ser Eustace's solar)
- **location_secondary:** Coldmoat (destination/referenced); Dorne (dream setting — red mountains, white sands); Ashford Meadow (recalled)
- **approximate_timeline:** Within this unit: one night (a cellar bath, then a nightmare on the roof), the following morning (a failed shield-wall drill, then a meeting in Ser Eustace's solar), and that evening (a second bath and planning). Ends with Dunk resolved to ride to Coldmoat "on the morrow."
- **time_markers:** "tonight"; "as the last kettle was coming to a boil"; "a good year and a half" (Egg's service to Dunk); "twenty" days (Dunk's hyperbole for Egg's service); "Come the break of day"; "The morning was already hot"; "upon the morrow" / "On the morrow"; "the next time the septon comes" (Will Barleycorn's wedding); "a fortnight" / "longer than a fortnight" (Osgrey sons' training of the levy); "five-and-twenty years" (Daeron II's reign); "nine-and-thirty" (Baelor's age at death); "a moon's turn"; "for a thousand years before the Conquest" (Osgreys as Marshalls); "one winter day" (Eustace's marriage proposal to Lord Wyman); "In a year, his bones will be scoured clean"; "two years as a page at King's Landing" (Egg); "for hundreds of years" (Targaryen sibling-marriage)
- **chapter_summary:** Dunk bathes in Standfast's undercellar with Egg's help while the two argue about the peasant levy Ser Eustace means to arm against the Widow's men; Dunk reflects on Egg's concealed identity as Aegon Targaryen. Sleeping on the roof, Dunk suffers a nightmare of digging graves in the Dornish sands for his dead horse Chestnut, his dead masters, and the doomed recruits. At dawn a shield-wall drill fails when the recruits break and flee before Dunk's charging warhorse, and Dunk reports to Ser Eustace that the levy will not serve. Eustace proposes paying Lady Webber a "blood price" to take down her dam and recounts the fallen history of House Osgrey and his vow never to enter Coldmoat, after which Dunk agrees to ride to Coldmoat himself to treat with the Red Widow. That evening Dunk takes a second bath while Egg relays rumors of the Red Widow's poisonings and black arts, and Dunk forbids Egg from accompanying him.
- **unit_part:** 3 of 8

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Severe drought; "The next time it rained on them up there would be the first." Savage heat by day.
- **Season indicators:** High summer / prolonged dry spell; "After the savage heat of the day"; "The morning was already hot"; a night sky thick with stars.
- **Time of day:** Night (first bath, then stargazing and the nightmare on the roof); dawn ("the break of day") for the drill; morning for the solar meeting; evening/night for the second bath.
- **Lighting:** The cellar is dim and lamplit ("In the dimness of the lamplit cellar"); Egg's eyes look black in the dimness but deep dark purple in better light. The solar is dim ("in the dimness of his solar"). Night roof lit by thousands of stars.
- **Sounds:** Splashing of bathwater; the empty kettle thumping onto the earthen floor; hens screeching and flapping under the warhorse; woven branches cracking and splintering under iron-shod hooves; Ser Bennis rattling off curses; a fat black fly buzzing.
- **Smells:** Ser Bennis "smelled like a bad cheese" (lice and fleas); Dunk fears turning up before Lady Webber "smelling like Ser Bennis"; Egg jokes of "a tub of Maester's droppings"; Dunk's sweat-stained tunic.
- **Notable sensory details:** Steam rising "in a white curtain" as hot water is poured; scalding bathwater cooler farther down; dirt coming off Dunk's arms "in long gray clouds"; water gone "gray and cold"; the dream's "dry hot earth" and "fine sand"; red froth bubbling on Bennis's lips; the sands filling Dunk's mouth, nose, and eyes.

## Characters Present
| Character | Role in Chapter | First Appearance? | Notes |
|-----------|----------------|-------------------|-------|
| Ser Duncan the Tall (Dunk) | POV; bathes, drills the levy, meets Ser Eustace, agrees to go to Coldmoat | No | "Dunk of Flea Bottom"; a hedge knight, now Ser Eustace's sworn sword |
| Egg | Dunk's squire; draws bathwater, argues over the levy, relays rumors | No | Revealed in-text as Aegon Targaryen |
| Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield | Drills the recruits in a shield wall at dawn; also appears in Dunk's nightmare | No | "crawls with lice and fleas and smelled like a bad cheese" |
| Ser Eustace Osgrey | Dunk's liege; meets him in the solar, proposes the blood price, recounts Osgrey history | No | A landed knight; the Osgreys were once Marshalls of the Northmarch |
| Big Rob | Recruit; "the first to drop his spear and run" in the drill; also a corpse in the nightmare | No | A simpleton; "doesn't even know his left foot from his right" |
| The eight recruits (Standfast levy) | Lined up shoulder to shoulder for the shield-wall drill; break and flee | No | Includes Wet Wat and Will Barleycorn (named); shields are woven wood |
| Ser Arlan of Pennytree | Dream apparition; reproaches Dunk for never weeping for him | No | Dunk's dead former master; died of a chill (dream/recalled) |
| Baelor Breakspear | Dream apparition; speaks from the grave | No | Dead prince; "the hope of Westeros" (dream/recalled) |
| Prince Valarr | Dream apparition; asks why the gods took his father and left Dunk | No | "the Young Prince," Baelor's son (dream/recalled) |
| Treb | Dream apparition; standing "with an arrow in his eye" | Yes (uncertain — verify) | Appears only as a corpse in the nightmare |
| Wet Wat | Dream apparition (corpse, head "cut near in half"); also named among the living recruits | No | A living recruit, "still half a boy"; dead only in the dream |
| Lem | Dream apparition ("old Lem"); a living Osgrey war-survivor | No | Came back from the war with Eustace; dead only in the dream |
| Pate | Dream apparition ("red-eyed Pate"); a living Osgrey war-survivor | No | Came back from the war with Eustace; dead only in the dream |

## Character Appearances
| Character | Hair | Eyes | Build/Height | Scars/Marks | Clothing/Armor | Weapons Worn | Distinguishing Features | Age (if stated) |
|-----------|------|------|-------------|-------------|----------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------|
| Egg | Shaven head (kept shaved or dyed); true hair "shone like beaten gold and strands of silver woven all together" | "deep and dark and purple" — Valyrian eyes; look black in dim light; "big eyes," made larger by the shaven head | A boy; "half a boy" is said of Wat, not Egg | Two hairs growing on his cheek, below the ear | — | — | Blushes "redder than a pomegranate"; shaven head rubbed for luck by orphan girls | Not stated; youngest of Maekar's four sons; served Dunk ~1½ years |
| Ser Duncan the Tall (Dunk) | Filthy hair (this night); once had "some fuzz growing on his upper lip" as a youth | — | Very tall and large; "It had been years since Dunk had met anyone as tall as he was"; "size and strength could make up for many lacks" | — | Sweat-stained tunic; a sandsilk "Dornish tunic" painted with his elm and falling star (his finest); chainmail worn all day | Longsword (kept sharp by Egg) | Poor with words and "worse with women" | — |
| Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield | — | — | — | — | Carries a brown shield (by his epithet) | — | "crawls with lice and fleas and smelled like a bad cheese"; chews sourleaf; red froth on his lips (dream) | — |
| Ser Eustace Osgrey | — | — | — | — | "my richest raiment" (recalled, for the marriage proposal) | — | Has a mustache that "quivered"; rubs his lip beneath it; an old knight | — |
| Prince Valarr (dream apparition) | "his father's light brown hair, but a streak of silver-gold ran through it" | "cool blue eyes" | — | — | — | — | "the Young Prince" | — |
| Baelor Breakspear (dream apparition) | — | — | — | — | — | — | "his prince, the hope of Westeros" | "only nine-and-thirty" at death |

## Characters Referenced
| Character | Context of Reference | Referenced By |
|-----------|---------------------|---------------|
| Aegon Targaryen | Egg's true name and identity; "went back to Summerhall with his brother Daeron after the tourney at Ashford Meadow" (the cover story) | Narration / Dunk |
| Maekar, Prince of Summerhall | Egg's father; fourth son of Daeron II; sent Egg to squire in secret; dealt the blow that killed Baelor (as "his brother") | Narration / Egg / Dunk |
| King Daeron the Good, the Second of His Name (Daeron II) | Egg's grandfather; sat the Iron Throne five-and-twenty years until the Great Spring Sickness took him | Narration |
| Daeron (Egg's brother) | Supposedly returned to Summerhall with Aegon after the Ashford tourney (the cover story) | Dunk |
| Lord Bloodraven | "How many eyes does Lord Bloodraven have? A thousand eyes, and one."; Shiera is his paramour | Dunk (internal) / Egg |
| Tanselle (Too-Tall) | Painted the falling star on Dunk's shield at Ashford; the "puppet girl" Dunk rescued from Aerion; "not too tall for me" | Dunk (internal) / Egg |
| Roger of Pennytree | "younger than Wet Wat when he died on the Redgrass Field" | Dunk |
| Sam Stoops | Told Dunk that Coldmoat was once the Osgreys'; reports Coldmoat's castellan is "as big as you are" | Dunk / Egg |
| Sam Stoops' wife | Soaks and beats the laundry; says the Red Widow "sold her babes unborn to the Lord of the Seven Hells" | Narration / Egg |
| Will Barleycorn | Recruit "to be married the next time the septon comes" | Egg |
| Lady Vaith | "was mad"; Dunk's words to her nearly got them killed in Dorne; "the only one you ever knew" | Egg / Dunk |
| Aerion | Dunk rescued the puppet girl from him; "Aerion's in Lys" | Egg / Dunk |

## Events & Actions
1. **The cellar bath** — Dunk bathes in the stone washtub while Egg draws, heats, and pours the water; they argue over the doomed levy and Egg's suppressed identity.
   - Agent: Ser Duncan (Dunk)
   - Patient: —
   - Instrument: washtub, well bucket, iron kettle, soap, long-handled horsehair scrub brush
   - Location: Standfast undercellar
   - Outcome: Dunk bathed; Egg sent to sleep
2. **Egg presses to "use the boot"** — Egg repeatedly urges deploying his concealed token to summon aid for the recruits; Dunk flatly refuses ("No.") each time.
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: Ser Duncan
   - Location: Standfast undercellar
   - Outcome: Refused; "the boot is only meant for dire need"
3. **Dunk recalls Egg's true identity** — Dunk reflects that his squire is Aegon Targaryen, youngest son of Maekar, and that Maekar bade Egg keep his head shaved or dyed and tell no man his true name.
   - Agent: Ser Duncan (internal)
   - Patient: Egg
   - Location: Standfast undercellar
   - Outcome: Identity established for the reader
4. **The nightmare on the roof** — Dunk dreams he is digging a grave in the Dornish sands for his dead horse Chestnut while apparitions of Ser Arlan, Baelor Breakspear, and Prince Valarr reproach him; then Big Rob, Ser Bennis, Treb, Wet Wat, Lem, and Pate appear as gutted corpses, and Bennis tells Dunk to dig "eight for them and one for me and one for old Ser Useless, and one last one for your bald-head boy"; the sands collapse and bury Egg and then Dunk.
   - Agent: Ser Duncan (dreamer)
   - Patient: Egg (buried in the dream); the recruits (dead in the dream)
   - Location: Standfast roof / Dornish sands (dream)
   - Outcome: Dunk wakes troubled
5. **The shield-wall drill fails** — At dawn Ser Bennis lines the eight recruits shoulder to shoulder with shields touching and spears out; Dunk and Egg charge on horseback; Egg's mount Maester balks, but Thunder charges home; Big Rob drops his spear and flees first and the rest follow, and Thunder tramples their discarded woven shields.
   - Agent: Ser Duncan (and Ser Bennis, drillmaster)
   - Patient: The eight recruits
   - Instrument: Thunder (warhorse); the recruits' woven-wood shields and spears
   - Location: Outside Standfast
   - Instigator: Ser Bennis (set the drill); Ser Eustace (whose war it is)
   - Witness: Egg ("Egg fought manfully to hold his laughter in, but finally lost the battle")
   - Outcome: Drill collapses; recruits scatter
6. **Dunk reports the failure to Ser Eustace** — In the solar Dunk tells Eustace the levy "will not serve"; Eustace defends them, recalling that only three of his men (Lem, Pate, Dake) came home from the war.
   - Agent: Ser Duncan
   - Patient: Ser Eustace Osgrey
   - Location: Ser Eustace's solar
   - Outcome: Eustace concedes it "may take longer than a fortnight"
7. **Ser Eustace proposes the blood price and tells the Osgrey history** — Eustace proposes paying Lady Webber a silver stag for the wounded man and three for the insult if she takes down the dam; he recounts the Osgreys as former Marshalls of the Northmarch, the loss of Coldmoat to King Maegor, Addam's rejected suit for Lord Wyman's daughter, and the drowning of Dake, and reveals his vow never to enter Coldmoat again "unless to take possession."
   - Agent: Ser Eustace Osgrey
   - Patient: Ser Duncan
   - Location: Ser Eustace's solar
   - Outcome: The blood-price plan set; Eustace cannot go himself
8. **Dunk agrees to ride to Coldmoat** — Dunk volunteers ("I swore no vows") to carry the offer to the Red Widow; Eustace praises him as "the true steel" and the man he had hoped his daughter Alysanne might wed; Dunk kneels and agrees to go "upon the morrow."
   - Agent: Ser Duncan
   - Patient: Ser Eustace Osgrey
   - Location: Ser Eustace's solar
   - Outcome: Dunk committed to the Coldmoat mission
9. **The second bath and the Red Widow rumors** — That evening Dunk bathes again to be presentable; Egg relays gossip that the Red Widow poisoned all four husbands, bore no living children, and deals in black arts, and jokes about gallantry, songs, and the Kingsguard; Dunk chooses his sandsilk Dornish tunic for the visit.
   - Agent: Ser Duncan / Egg
   - Location: Standfast undercellar
   - Outcome: Preparations made
10. **Dunk forbids Egg to come to Coldmoat** — Egg argues a knight is more impressive with a squire; Dunk, fearing the danger (recalling the two men in crow cages), orders Egg to stay and help Bennis with the smallfolk.
    - Agent: Ser Duncan
    - Patient: Egg
    - Location: Standfast undercellar
    - Outcome: "You're not going, and that's the end of it."

## POV Character's Internal State
- **Emotional state:** Weary and grief-haunted (the nightmare of dead masters, his dead horse, and the doomed recruits leaves him "soiled and sticky as if he'd never bathed"); affectionate toward Egg ("almost as a little brother"); apprehensive about the Coldmoat mission and daunted more by "the prospect of facing the Red Widow" than by the giant Lucas Longinch; quietly flattered and reddening at Eustace's praise and talk of Alysanne.
- **Primary preoccupation:** The coming confrontation over the dam — how to treat with Lady Webber without begging, how to keep the doomed recruits and Egg from being killed, and rehearsing what to say to a highborn lady.
- **Key decisions made:** Refuses to let Egg "use the boot"; agrees to ride to Coldmoat to offer the blood price and ask for the dam's removal; will wear his sandsilk Dornish tunic; forbids Egg to accompany him and orders him to stay and help Bennis with the smallfolk.
- **Self-deception flags:** Tells the recruits' deaths are "their duty... to die, if need be" and "The same thing, only smaller" while privately thinking "this is madness" and "they will soon be dead lads, if they go up against the knights of Coldmoat." Insists to himself of the dream, "It never happened that way... I never wept," while admitting "I might have wanted to." Downplays his dread of the Red Widow as "I'm going to talk to the Red Widow, that's all."

input.content:
# TSS — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** TSS
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Coldmoat (arrival, outer ward, and the crowd outside the castle sept)
- **location_secondary:** The west way between Standfast and Coldmoat; Wat's Wood; the Chequy Water crossing; Standfast (departure); Redgrass Field and Weeping Ridge (recounted battle)
- **approximate_timeline:** A single continuous stretch within this unit: dawn departure from Standfast; the morning ride down the west way, during which Ser Eustace recounts the Redgrass Field and then turns back at a tree-cat carcass in Wat's Wood; the crossing of the Chequy Water into the Widow's lands; arrival at Coldmoat "several hours later" (afternoon); and the confrontation in the outer ward culminating in the meeting with Lady Rohanne. Ends with Dunk granted an audience.
- **time_markers:** "the light of the morning sun in his face"; "how can it be so hot so soon?"; "Even at this hour, with the sun barely above the horizon"; "By afternoon the saddles will be hot enough to raise blisters"; "these past years" (the west way little used); "Before the Conquest" / "in my father's day" (Wat's Wood); "several hours later" (spied the towers of Coldmoat); "before the sun has set"; "all morning" (Rohanne's devotions); "two years past" (the Great Spring Sickness); "when I was ten" / "He was twelve" (Rohanne's first marriage); "The last died in the spring"; "Centuries of wind and weather" (the worn gate sigil); "for near an hour" (Daemon and Gwayne Corbray)
- **chapter_summary:** Ser Eustace outfits Dunk with a green-and-gold cloak, assigns Egg to accompany him as squire, and rides part of the way toward Coldmoat, recounting the Battle of the Redgrass Field and his belief that Bloodraven's sorcery, not Baelor Breakspear, won it. Eustace turns back at a tree-cat carcass in Wat's Wood, and Dunk and Egg cross the Chequy Water into Lady Webber's lands, Egg coaching Dunk on gallant compliments. At Coldmoat Dunk is met with scorn by the castellan Ser Lucas Longinch and by the drunken Septon Sefton, then embarrasses himself by mistaking the stout Lady Helicent for the Red Widow before the mocking crowd. The true Red Widow — Lady Rohanne Webber, a freckled young archer of five-and-twenty — reveals herself, teases Dunk through his fumbled gallantries, examines the elm-and-star arms painted on his tunic, and grants him a private audience, sending for Maester Cerrick and for Dunk's squire.
- **unit_part:** 4 of 8

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Severe, oppressive heat and drought; "how can it be so hot so soon?"; hot even at dawn; "By afternoon the saddles will be hot enough to raise blisters"; "the ward is too hot and dusty"; "beneath this beastly sun."
- **Season indicators:** High summer / prolonged dry spell; the moat has fallen six feet at least; the diverted stream is used to water crops in the fields.
- **Time of day:** Dawn departure; morning ride; afternoon arrival at Coldmoat.
- **Lighting:** Bright, harsh sun ("shining green and golden in the sun"); the diverted ditch "shining green and golden"; leaded-glass windows of the sept.
- **Sounds:** Singing from the sept; hounds barking in the kennels; the gurgle of falling water into the moat; the whir of the spinning quintain; the smith shoeing a warhorse; laughter and japes of the crowd; the septon's fart.
- **Smells:** The maggot-crawling carcass of a tree cat ("that stinks worse than Ser Bennis"); the guards joke of smelling Bennis coming.
- **Notable sensory details:** "Even the flies have fled"; a thin finger of sweat creeping down Dunk's chest; the septon "sweating worse than Dunk... as damp as if he'd worn them in his bath"; the diverted water "ran straight as a spear, shining green and golden"; the worn chequy-lion sigil above Coldmoat's gate.
